betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Crafting Table§f is the §6single block§f that makes §6every AR multiblock machine§f — without it, you cannot build a Lathe, Rolling Machine, Precision Assembler, or any other AR machine. It is the §6first machine§f the AR progression asks the player to build, and it gates every other machine in the mod.

§6Production Path (platepress.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  . . .
  . a .
  i i i
  ```
- Key: `a`=§6minecraft:piston§f, `i`=§6ingotIron§f (forge:ore_dict, 3 ingots in the bottom row)
- Output: 1x §6Crafting Table§f (advancedrocketry:platepress)
- Power: passive; the presser is always active

The presser is used by placing a §6machine block§f (the input machine you want to build) above the presser and a §6Block of Obsidian§f below the presser. When the presser receives a §6redstone pulse§f, it converts the machine block above into a §6fully assembled multiblock§f by consuming the §6machine structure block§f (libvulpes:structuremachine) from the input. The output is the §6assembled multiblock controller§f plus any leftover items.

The presser is the §6prerequisite§f for the §6Lathe§f (10010), §6Rolling Machine§f (10011), §6Precision Assembler§f (10012), §6Centrifuge§f (10018), §6Electrolyser§f (10016), §6Chemical Reactor§f (10017), and the §6Oxygen Vent§f (10051). It is the §6most-used§f AR block in the entire mod.

§eTip§f: Build at least 2 §6Crafting Tables§f in your base — one for machine assembly, one for plate crafting. Pair with a §6Hopper§f above the presser to auto-supply the machine block from a chest. The §6Block of Obsidian§f below the presser is §6consumable§f on use; build an obsidian farm.
